sentence: The patient, a 56-year-old male, presents with a 4-5 week history of difficulty swallowing.
He reports feeling like something is stuck in his throat, which is occasionally painful, and he has to wash down solid food to prevent choking.
He denies coughing, blood in stool or vomit, weight loss, chest pain, or shortness of breath.
He reports that the difficulty swallowing is more pronounced with solid food, and not with liquids.
He has a past medical history of diabetes and high blood pressure, for which he is on metformin 500mg twice daily and norvasc.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure, for which he is on metformin 500mg twice daily and norvasc.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms, including nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or other side effects from his medications.
Norvasc [dosage not specified] [frequency not specified] for high blood pressure
The patient's neck is fine with no swelling.
There are no masses in the neck.
The throat looks okay with no masses or swelling.
The heart exam is normal with no murmurs.
The belly exam shows epigastric tenderness on the right side.
Assessment: The patient presents with difficulty swallowing, which may be due to a narrowing in the throat, possibly a stricture of the esophagus.
Problem list: Dysphagia (active); Diabetes mellitus (active); Hypertension (active)
I will send the patient to a gastroenterology specialist for a scope to investigate the narrowing in the throat.
I will also prescribe prilosec 20mg once daily for gastroesophageal reflux disease.
I will recommend that the patient chew their food frequently, especially hard foods like steak or meat, to prevent getting stuck.
I will order another hemoglobin A1c and blood work to check kidney function.
I will also refer the patient to ophthalmology for an eye examination to check for diabetic retinopathy.
I will continue the patient on metformin and norvasc.
I will see the patient back in about a month to review the results of the blood tests and make any necessary adjustments to their medication.
